channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump wants debt ceiling in the budget deal. If not, he says let the government shutdown start now
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Hours before the start of a federal government shutdown, President-elect Donald Trump doubled-down Friday on his insistence that a debt ceiling increase be included in any deal — and if not, let the closures “start now.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congress scrambles to reach funding deal
item.description: The government is less than 24 hours away from a shutdown, unless Congress can accomplish a Christmas miracle in the 11th hour to fund the government.
item.dc:creator: Basil John, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Johnson says plan C reached to avert shutdown, vote expected
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Republicans have arrived at a plan C to avert a shutdown and the House will vote Friday morning on the legislation. "Yeah, yeah, we have a plan," Johnson said Friday morning as he entered the Capitol. "We're expecting votes this morning, so you all stay tuned.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s reelection victory pushes some LGTBQ Minneapolis residents to safety training
item.description: For some marginalized communities the second Trump term is fraught with fears over personal safety. In Minneapolis, one organization is helping with de-escalation services and gun training.
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef, MPR News

item.title: Internal divisions doom Michigan Democrats in their final days of legislative control
item.description: Michigan Democrats are ending their final days in control of the state government bogged down with disunity and inaction.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Volmert And Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden-Harris admin rolls out another $4.28 billion in student loan handouts
item.description: As the Biden administration enters its final days, it has announced another $4.28 billion in student loan handouts for nearly 54,900 public workers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House starts scrapping pending regulations, including student debt cancellation
item.description: President Joe Biden’s administration has started to withdraw pending regulations that won’t be finalized by the time he leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Here's a list of the 38 Republicans who defied Trump by voting 'no' on the failed spending bill
item.description: A bill to avert a partial government shutdown that was backed by President-elect Trump failed to pass the House of Representatives on Thursday night, pushing Congress closer to a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In an early legislative test for Trump, plan B spending bill tanks in House
item.description: House Republicans failed to secure the votes needed Thursday on a spending bill amid a looming government shutdown, handing a loss to President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luna wants disaster aid separate from government funding bill
item.description: Hurricane survivors will need to wait a while longer to see additional disaster relief aid come from Congress.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to know about the debt ceiling debate as a government shutdown looms in Washington
item.description: A debate over the debt ceiling is at the center of a dispute over funding that is pushing Washington to the brink of a federal government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon warns that a government shutdown will cost troops their pay over the holidays
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans’ decision to abandon a congressional spending plan will cost troops their paychecks over the holidays unless some agreement is reached before Friday's deadline to prevent a government shutdown, the Pentagon warned.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House says Biden will visit Pope Francis, Meloni in final international trip of his presidency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden will travel to Italy next month to meet with Pope Francis and top Italian officials for what is expected to be the final international trip of his presidency.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New House GOP budget proposal revives Farm Bill extension and aid money but drops ethanol provision
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — American farmers are hoping that aid to agriculture will be revived as Congress struggles to pass a short-term spending bill that would keep the federal government funded and avert a looming partial government shutdown set to begin after midnight Friday night.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump-backed spending bill goes down in flames as shutdown looms
item.description: The House of Representatives is in chaos amid a last-minute bid to avert a Friday government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump backs plan B spending deal, urges swift passage
item.description: “All Republicans, and even the Democrats, should do what is best for our Country, and vote ‘YES’ for this Bill, TONIGHT!“ Trump posted on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Dems say 'hell no' to GOP plan B on government funding, debt ceiling
item.description: House Democrats are rallying in overwhelming opposition to the Republicans' second stab at a government funding bill, vowing to sink it on the House floor and force GOP leaders back to the table to negotiate a bipartisan compromise.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House rejects Trump-backed plan on government shutdown, leaving next steps uncertain
item.description: With a resounding no, the House rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s new plan Thursday to fund federal operations and suspend the debt ceiling a day before a government shutdown, as Democrats and dozens of Republicans refused to accommodate his sudden demands.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump backs plan B spending deal, urges swift passage
item.description: President-elect Trump on Thursday backed a new spending deal brokered by House Republicans to avert a shutdown after an initial proposal was torpedoed amid opposition from Trump and several of his allies.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FAA bans drones in parts of New Jersey
item.description: The FAA warned that "deadly force" could be used against the drones if they pose an "imminent security threat.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Advocates seek to prepare for possible ‘mass deportations’ without fearmongering
item.description: Minnesota immigrant rights groups say they want to avoid evoking fear in their communities, but are finding ways to stay prepared ahead of the next Trump presidency.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Gov Abbott unveils new campaign exposing horrific dangers of illegal immigration
item.description: Texas Gov. Abbott announced a new billboard campaign warning about the “horrific" reality of the human trafficking, violence and danger facing migrants if they attempt to illegally enter the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics: Musing About a Speaker Musk
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What a potential government shutdown could mean for San Diego
item.description: The federal government is barreling towards a partial shutdown after GOP lawmakers in Congress backed away from a deal on a short-term spending bill struck with Democrats just days before the Friday deadline.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Chinese national charged with acting as Beijing's agent in local California election
item.description: A Chinese national has been charged with acting as an illegal agent for Beijing when serving as the campaign manager for an unnamed local California politician.
item.dc:creator: Didi Tang, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Blinken announces aid for Sudan and talks about AI risks at UN Security Council
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken announced $200 million in humanitarian aid for conflict-torn Sudan during what was likely his final trip to the United Nations while in office. With the U.N. Security Council more divided than ever, Blinken led two meetings of the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota hastens to regulate helium mining after deposit discovery near Babbitt
item.description: Minnesota is scrambling to figure out how to regulate a potential new industry — one discovered by accident — that is both extremely valuable and in demand.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Vance meets with Johnson after Trump torpedoes spending bill
item.description: Vice President-elect JD Vance met with Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Wednesday night, hours after President-elect Trump torpedoed the GOP leader’s spending deal.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries suggests Democrats will oppose a 'clean' funding bill after Johnson's plan falters
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) suggested Wednesday that Democrats will oppose any federal spending bill that strays from the bipartisan deal announced a day prior, accusing GOP leaders of reneging on the agreement and risking a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Donald Trump said he wants to ban trans athletes from competing. The reality is more nuanced
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s campaign promise to ‘ban’ transgender athletes struck a chord with voters. It’s uncertain, however, what exactly Trump will do once his second term begins in January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: A power grab by Republicans in North Carolina becomes a referendum on democracy in the states
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — Democrats in North Carolina were celebrating big wins in the swing state after the November election, including victories in races for governor and other top statewide offices. But the political high didn’t last long.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO and GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former top aide to Mayor Adams surrenders to DA's office to face charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former top aide to New York City Mayor Eric Adams surrendered to authorities Thursday to face corruption charges in the latest blow to the administration of a mayor who has seen who is facing his own indictment and investigations into multiple aides and allies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump's words of opposition stop a bipartisan budget deal in its tracks with Musk's help
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump delivered a likely death blow to bipartisan congressional budget negotiations, rejecting the measure as full of giveaways to Democrats after billionaire ally Elon Musk whipped up outrage toward the bill and cheered on Republican lawmakers who announced
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ryan Routh, alleged would-be Trump assassin, faces new felony charge
item.description: Florida Attorney General Ashley Moody obtained an arrest warrant for Ryan Routh on an unrelated charge of attempted felony murder involving a six-year-old in a car accident.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Party switch paying off? Valdes secures committee leadership after joining GOP
item.description: One state Representative in the Tampa Bay area made the controversial move to switch political parties, just last week. The news came was greeted with both cheers and criticisms.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former top aide to Mayor Adams expected in court on criminal charges: sources
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former top aide to New York City Mayor Eric Adams is expected in court Thursday to face corruption charges in the latest blow to an administration beset by searches, resignations and the mayor's own indictment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Global warming can’t be ignored, Montana’s top court says, upholding landmark climate case
item.description: Montana’s Supreme Court has upheld a landmark climate ruling that said the state was violating residents’ constitutional right to a clean environment by permitting oil, gas and coal projects without regard for global warming.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
